generated_oml:
instance LivingLabAcademicBuilding : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "A LEED Platinum-certified academic building serving as a testbed for real-world Digital Twin applications, emphasizing energy efficiency, indoor environmental quality, and operational optimization in a Living Lab environment"
    base:contains LivingLabBuildingManagementSystem,
                 RealTimeEnvironmentalSensorNetwork,
                 ModelDrivenEngineeringFramework
]

instance BuildingAutomationActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuators interfacing with building systems (e.g., HVAC, lighting, valves) for operational control, enabled through the Digital Twin framework's bidirectional communication capabilities"
]

instance IndoorAirQualitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring sensor for indoor environmental parameters (CO2, VOCs, temperature, humidity) integrated into the building's sensor network"
    DTDFVocab:producedData IndoorAirQualityDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralHealthMonitoringSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor network for structural integrity monitoring (vibration, strain, displacement) to support predictive maintenance and operational safety"
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralHealthDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionMeter : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision energy meters tracking real-time consumption across building systems (HVAC, lighting, equipment) for operational optimization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancyPresenceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Occupancy detection sensors (PIR, thermal imaging) providing real-time occupancy data for adaptive building control and energy management"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance IndoorAirQualityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IndoorAirQualitySensor
]

instance StructuralHealthDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralHealthMonitoringSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionMeter
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancyPresenceSensor
]

instance BimBased3DModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically rich Building Information Model (BIM) incorporating geometric, spatial, and functional characteristics of building components with IFC or proprietary formats"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoDataIngestion,
                 EclipseDittoSynchronization
]

instance PhysicsBasedSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based model for building behavior simulation under varying conditions (occupancy, weather, system failures) supporting energy optimization and control strategy validation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineeringFramework
]

instance OperationalDataRepository : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Centralized repository storing real-time operational data (sensor readings, equipment status, environmental metrics) synchronized via Eclipse Hono and Ditto"
    DTDFVocab:fromData IndoorAirQualityDataTransmitted,
                       StructuralHealthDataTransmitted,
                       EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted,
                       OccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoDataIngestion,
                 EclipseDittoSynchronization
]

instance HistoricalPerformanceDataset : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Longitudinal dataset capturing building performance across design, construction, and operational phases for lifecycle analysis and model refinement"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineeringFramework
]

instance EclipseHonoDataIngestion : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source IoT data ingestion platform enabling real-time device connectivity and protocol adaptation (AMQP, MQTT) for building sensor networks"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService,
                 DataSynchronizationService
]

instance EclipseDittoSynchronization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital Twin state management framework providing live synchronization between physical assets and their virtual representations via asset administration shells"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService,
                 DataSynchronizationService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering framework enabling abstraction, simulation, and lifecycle management of Digital Twin models through automated model transformations"
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService,
                 LifecycleOptimizationService,
                 ModelRefinementService
]

instance EclipseBaSyxPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source Industry 4.0 platform providing modular architecture and interoperability through OPC UA integration and asset administration shells"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService,
                 SimulationService,
                 VisualizationService
]

instance ItwinJsLibrary : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Web-based library for developing infrastructure Digital Twin applications with real-time synchronization capabilities for large-scale collaborative projects"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance UnrealEngineRenderer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-fidelity 3D visualization engine enabling immersive Digital Twin experiences with real-time rendering of building performance data"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous data ingestion and synchronization between physical building and Digital Twin representation, enabling operational awareness for facility managers and researchers"
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingOperationalInsight,
                 DataSynchronizationAction
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Execution of physics-based and data-driven simulations to model building behavior under varying conditions (occupancy, weather, system failures)"

                 SystemDegradationPredictionInsight,
                 ControlStrategyValidationInsight
]

instance LifecycleOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integration of design, construction, and operational phases into a unified Digital Twin framework to optimize long-term performance, cost, and sustainability"
    DTDFVocab:provides LifecyclePerformanceInsight,

]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D and data-driven visualizations for stakeholders to analyze building performance, identify anomalies, and support decision-making"
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveBuildingVisualizationInsight
]

instance DataSynchronizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated synchronization mechanism ensuring real-time consistency between physical assets and their Digital Twin representations"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataConsistencyInsight
]

instance ModelRefinementService : DTDFVocab:Service [

    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
]

instance BuildingOperationalInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [

]

]

]

    base:desc "Validation results for building control strategies through comparative analysis of simulated vs. actual system performance"
]

    base:desc "Automated synchronization of sensor data and equipment status between physical building and Digital Twin"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ContinuousModelRefinementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance LivingLabBuildingManagementSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Core operational control and data acquisition hub for the LEED Platinum academic building, integrating BMS functionality with Digital Twin capabilities"
    base:isContainedIn LivingLabAcademicBuilding
]

instance RealTimeEnvironmentalSensorNetwork : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Highly instrumented sensor network providing real-time monitoring of environmental and structural parameters across the building"
    base:isContainedIn LivingLabAcademicBuilding
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringFrameworkComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Framework enabling abstraction, simulation, and lifecycle management of Digital Twin models through model-driven engineering principles"
    base:isContainedIn LivingLabAcademicBuilding
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system enables **control flows** from the digital to the physical domain, primarily through the role of **Facility Managers**, who interact with the deployed DT for **real-time monitoring and control** of physical infrastructure (e.g., lighting systems, broader operational systems). The framework supports **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic adjustments to control logic without requiring full redeployment. While the document does not specify **command types** (e.g., HVAC adjustments, security protocols) or **control message formats**, the system is designed to ensure **bidirectional data exchange** between the DT and physical assets. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** for control commands are not explicitly described, nor are **feedback systems** or **conflict resolution** strategies detailed. However, the framework’s **modular architecture** implies support for standardized pathways to execute commands, with **command execution confirmation** inferred through the continuous synchronization loop. The absence of explicit technical details (e.g., message schemas, encryption protocols) limits granularity."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, enabling near-instantaneous bidirectional updates. While the document does not specify exact synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms), the emphasis on **runtime configuration management** implies dynamic adjustments to operational needs, suggesting adaptive temporal granularity for different services (e.g., sensor data, control signals, or analytics). Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the **interactive visualization** and **real-time monitoring/control** use cases, where delays must align with human operational workflows (e.g., Facility Managers interacting with the DT). The framework’s modular architecture supports **standardized extension points**, allowing custom services to define their own temporal constraints (e.g., high-frequency sensor fusion vs. periodic energy analytics)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **coordinated orchestration pattern** centered on the **DT Framework**, which serves as a unifying hub for **bidirectional data exchange** and **holistic building life cycle management**. The architecture follows a **role-based component integration** model, where **Building Control Engineers**, **Facility Managers**, and **DT Engineers** contribute to distinct phases of the DT life cycle (deployment, monitoring, and extension). **Resource management** is implicit in the **runtime configuration management** capability, enabling dynamic adjustments without full redeployment, while **system-wide coordination** is achieved through the framework’s **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points**. The **enriched BIM-to-operational DT flow** (as depicted in Figure 1) ensures alignment between static design data and dynamic operational needs, with the framework facilitating interoperability across heterogeneous components."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans **five core phases**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. These phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** directly within the BIM environment, enriching static BIM models with **semantic representations** of Building Control System (BCS) components. This phase establishes a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations.
  2. **Model Transformation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts **structured representations** from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention. This phase ensures seamless transition from static BIM to dynamic DT representations.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed, enabling real-time synchronization between the physical building and its digital twin. This phase includes **initialization of control logic** and **integration of core DT services** as defined by Building Control Engineers.
  4. **Operation and Monitoring**: Facility Managers interact with the DT to **monitor system states** and **operate physical infrastructure** via the twin. This phase emphasizes **real-time data exchange** and **interactive visualization** for immersive monitoring and control.
  5. **Evolution and Extension**: DT Engineers extend functionality through **custom services and plugins**, integrated via the framework’s **modular architecture** with standardized extension points. This phase supports **runtime evolution** of the DT to adapt to changing operational requirements.

  **Transition Management**: The framework ensures **automated synchronization** between phases, particularly between device modeling (BIM) and DT deployment, using MDE-driven transformations. **Lifecycle governance** is implicit in the roles defined (Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers), each responsible for distinct phases, ensuring accountability and consistency. The modular architecture further supports **version control** and **evolution management** by isolating custom services and analytics from core DT functionality."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework ensures **semantic fidelity** through BIM’s object-based modeling, which inherently supports clash detection, performance simulation, and documentation alignment. Validation is implicitly supported by the **real-time synchronization** (via Eclipse Hono/Ditto) and the **modular architecture**, which allows for iterative refinement of models. The system prioritizes **semantic integration** and **operational alignment** between design-time (BIM) and runtime models, though explicit validation methods (e.g., statistical testing, uncertainty quantification) or error correction mechanisms are not detailed. Quality assurance is embedded in the **model-driven deployment process**, ensuring reproducibility and scalability in AECO contexts."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system’s **technical network infrastructure** is underpinned by a **modular architecture** with standardized extension points, enabling integration of custom services and analytics. The document highlights **real-time data exchange capabilities**, suggesting the use of **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** (though their specific roles—e.g., device connectivity vs. state management—are not defined). The system prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems**, implying support for **cross-platform communication protocols** (e.g., MQTT, HTTP/REST) and **scalable network topologies** to accommodate components from individual devices to entire infrastructure systems. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are not quantified, but the emphasis on real-time synchronization implies low-latency, high-throughput pathways. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not explicitly described, though the framework’s adaptability suggests fault-tolerant design. **Security protocols** for network communication are implied by the need for interoperability and data integrity but are not detailed."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) framework supports scalable deployment models, with a modular architecture enabling integration of custom services and analytics. While the document does not explicitly mention hosting platforms (e.g., cloud, edge, or on-premise), the emphasis on **scalability** and **runtime configuration management** suggests adaptability to varying deployment scenarios. Computational and storage requirements are tailored to the operational needs of the AECO domain, with low-overhead updates ensuring maintainability. The framework’s central hub role facilitates bidirectional data exchange, implying a cohesive deployment strategy, but specific details about availability, reliability assurance, or deployment models (e.g., hybrid) are not provided in the document."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** as a non-functional requirement (NF_X), ensuring seamless integration with external tools and platforms. Key integration aspects include:
  - **Data exchange protocols**: Leverages **Eclipse Hono** (for IoT data ingestion) and **Eclipse Ditto** (for digital twin state management) to standardize communication.
  - **Dependencies**: Relies on **BIM tools** (e.g., Revit) for initial device modeling and enriched model deployment, while operational data flows through the DT Framework.
  - **Integration patterns**: Supports **modular service extension**, allowing third-party systems (e.g., enterprise asset management, CMMS) to connect via standardized interfaces. The framework does not specify exact protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, REST APIs) but emphasizes **scalable, low-overhead interoperability** to bridge static BIM representations with dynamic operational needs in the AECO domain."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to **AECO-specific standards** (e.g., **BIM**) while addressing **interoperability and modularity challenges** through **standardized extension points**. **(1) Industry Standards**: The solution is built upon **BIM as a foundational standard**, enabling seamless integration with **Building Information Modeling (BIM) tools** and **Building Control System (BCS) components**. **(2) Compliance Frameworks**: The framework’s **modular architecture** incorporates **standardized extension points** to ensure compatibility with **third-party services and plugins**, mitigating the lack of standardized integration frameworks in traditional BIM-DT deployments. **(3) Interoperability Standards**: While specific standards (e.g., IFC, OPC UA) are not explicitly named, the **automated MDE pipelines** and **real-time data exchange** imply adherence to **industry protocols for bidirectional communication** between physical and digital systems. **(4) Certification Requirements**: No explicit certification requirements are detailed, but the **role-based governance model** (e.g., Building Control Engineers, DT Engineers) suggests alignment with **industry best practices for DT deployment and operation**. The framework’s **semantic enrichment of BIM models** ensures **consistent data representation**, addressing the **static limitations of BIM** while maintaining compliance with **AECO workflows**."
]
